comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report, 2:17 PM

Good afternoon, Sugarloafers,

We might not have been able to see the trails for the first hour of the day, but they were there, and they were good. And when the fog cleared, we could see the six inches of snow groomed out to soft, carvable turns. The groomers were able to loosen the firm surface, providing us with some really enjoyable skiing conditions.

The weekend isn't over yet. Snow is forecast for tomorrow, starting around 7 AM and accumulating throughout the day. It looks like by the time lifts close tomorrow, we'll have grabbed onto about 8 inches of snow, and it will continue into the evening. By midday Monday there should be almost a foot of accumulation.

For the rest of the weather, temperatures will stay in the 20s across the mountain, with another round of mild wind.

Lifts tomorrow are: West Mountain and Snubber at 8 AM. King Pine Superquad, DRC-B, Sawduster, Skyline, Bucksaw, Skidway, and Whiffletree at 8:30 AM. Moosecalator at 9 AM.

King Pine will be closing at 3 PM due to the Cross-Haul egress out of the King Pine Bowl being closed.

For passholders with first tracks access, Superquad and Skyline will be your lifts for tomorrow.

Groomers will be getting all the usuals tonight, and maaaay even try to sneak in a pass at Choker and Misery Whip. Stay tuned to see if this rumor is true.

It's Après all day thanks to our spring DJ series. From 12-3 PM, DJ J-Bird will be on the beach, and DJ H.L.H ft. DJ Rice Pilaf will be at Bullwinkles. After, head to Widowmaker from 3-6 PM, featuring music by Crowded Table Band.

Remember, 2026/27 New England Season Passes are now available. But wait, there's more. 2025/26 season pass holders and ticket pack holders can take advantage of the biggest savings of the year during the Renewal Flash Sale on qualifying purchases made before Thursday, April 2. Grab everything through our quick links.

Stay stoked,
Shannon

comment.name: Uphill
comment.text:
Uphill travel is open to Bullwinkles via the West Mountain Route and Superquad Route.

Visit the Uphill Policy page for detailed directions.

Note: All day-ticket uphill travelers must pick up their 1-day wristband prior to starting their ascent with Guest Services located in the base lodge. Your wristband must be visible at all times. For more information, visit the Uphill Policy page.
